title: Generalize the registry seam to a config-declared toml source descriptor
description:
Today the registry-first (`.toml`) source path is wired to exactly one registry: `InvariantRegistry::load(.jit/invariants.toml)` (config.rs:1232/1271), selected by a hard `kind.name()==INVARIANT_KIND_NAME` guard (commands/item.rs:202); any other registry-first kind errors as `UnknownRegistrySource` (commands/item.rs:205-212). The `source` field is markdown-path-only `Option<String>` (config.rs:326-332).

Scope: extend the kind `source` to express a structured toml descriptor (toml path + table + self-id field + text field + link-namespace fields). A generic loader builds `RawScopeItem` rows from that descriptor and feeds them into `index_project_sources` (item.rs:1155-1169), superseding `invariant_raw_items`. Keep `InvariantRegistry` (validation/invariants.rs) as the typed/validated invariant loader. All toml IO goes through the storage boundary (`read_repo_file`), not `std::fs`. New public surfaces carry `# Examples`, typed thiserror errors.

## Success Criteria
- [hard] REQ-02: A non-invariant registry-first kind is backed by a custom `.toml` through the declared field mapping (self-id, text, link-namespace fields), verified by a test indexing that kind from the custom toml; the toml read goes through the storage boundary and the existing invariant indexing path still passes.
